channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Meta Paid $78,000 Bounty for Vulnerability Exposing Customer Support Data
item.description: A security researcher discovered a broken access control vulnerability in Meta’s support infrastructure. The post Meta Paid $78,000 Bounty for Vulnerability Exposing Customer Support Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors across models
item.description: Jul 21, 10:11 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating issues with Claude Haiku 4.5 and Opus 4.8.

item.title: Audit Logs delayed
item.description: Jul 21, 10:06 UTC Investigating - We are currently seeing a delay in making new audit logs available for retrieval. This is restricted to the new audit logs v2 system, v1 audit logs are unaffected. No logs are being lost.

item.title: Clover Health Investments Discloses Data Breach
item.description: Using social engineering, hackers compromised employee accounts with access to personal and health information. The post Clover Health Investments Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
item.description: Attackers have begun to exploit two critical vulnerabilities in WordPress that, when combined together, enable unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) and complete compromise of vulnerable websites. The two security flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137, have been codenamed...

item.title: Elevated errors on Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 21, 08:53 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 21, 08:19 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jul 21, 07:11 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Exploitation of ServiceNow Vulnerability Seen Days After Disclosure
item.description: The ServiceNow AI platform vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-6875 can be exploited for remote code execution. The post Exploitation of ServiceNow Vulnerability Seen Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Zimbra Update Patches Critical Vulnerabilities
item.description: The latest Zimbra refresh resolves command injection, XSS, restriction bypass, and SSRF security defects. The post Zimbra Update Patches Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Zhipu AI Computing Ambitions Surface: 1GW Data Center, Hundreds of Millions in Infrastructure Acquisition, and Self-Developed Chip Plans Signal Nvidia Independence Push
item.description: Zhipu AI builds 1GW data center with all domestic chips, acquires compiler startup Zhongke Jiahe, and explores custom AI chip development, accelerating its decoupling from Nvidia ecosystem.

item.title: XPower Technology Defies Big-Chip Orthodoxy: Small RPP Architecture Chips Use Distributed Computing to Beat Giants at AI Inference
item.description: XPower Technology unveils RPP architecture AE7100E chips at WAIC: fingernail-sized chips with 12 on one card, supporting 400B-1.6TB parameter models through distributed computing at lowest token cost.

item.title: Tencent ProLaViT Teaches Multimodal LLMs to Reason Step-by-Step in Latent Space, Ending Swallowing-Whole Visual Reasoning Failures
item.description: Tencent BAC team proposes ProLaViT framework for progressive latent visual thought, enabling multimodal LLMs to reason step-by-step in latent space without external vision tools, accepted at ECCV 2026.

item.title: LONGi Green Energy Plants a Technology Forest on BC Cells: ACM Silver-Free Contact, Integrated Conductive Backsheet Lead Beyond 690W Modules
item.description: LONGi Green Energy unveils eight innovations including ACM nano-alloy contact replacing silver, 672W ACM module, 690W stackable BC technology, and 35.5% crystalline-silicon-perovskite tandem cell world record.

item.title: AI Chip Cooling Breakthrough: Scientists Create Theta-TaN Metal With 3x Copper Thermal Conductivity, Ending a Century-Long Record
item.description: UCLA and Tohoku University create theta-phase TaN single crystal with 1,100 W/mK thermal conductivity, approaching diamond-level heat dissipation with metal process compatibility for AI chip packaging.

item.title: New ENCFORGE Ransomware Targets AI Model Files in Langflow RCE Attack
item.description: Researchers at Sysdig have linked a second attack on the same Langflow server to JADEPUFFER, the AI-agent-driven operator it first documented earlier this month. The same operator has now been spotted deploying ENCFORGE, a new compiled Go ransomware designed to encrypt model weights, vector...

item.title: Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
item.description: Threat actors are now exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting ServiceNow AI Platform, according to Defused Cyber. In a post shared on X, the threat intelligence firm said it's observing in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-6875 (CVSS score: 9.5), a sandbox escape...

item.title: Rater State Bias in RLHF Preference Data: An Audit Framework
item.description: arXiv:2607.16195v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We identify a structured confound in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). Pairwise preference labels are intended to reflect the compared outputs, but they may also reflect the rater's state during annotation. Under sustained stressful...

item.title: Design and Validation of a Lightweight 1D CNN for Affective Touch Classification in Soft Plush Companions
item.description: arXiv:2607.16196v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Soft, sensorized companions offer a physically safe and emotionally intuitive interface for socially assistive technologies, yet their deformability and multichannel tactile sensing complicate the robust interpretation of human affect. This study...

item.title: Some Large Language Models Exhibit Consistent Risk Attitudes
item.description: arXiv:2607.16197v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As artificial intelligence systems are deployed in open-ended, high-stakes settings, a critical dimension remains unmeasured: how perceived risk is translated into action. We test whether large language models (LLMs) exhibit systematic and consistent...

item.title: A Survey on GNN-based Link Prediction: Techniques, Applications, and Challenges
item.description: arXiv:2607.16198v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have emerged as the leading paradigm for link prediction, enabling the inference of missing connections and the anticipation of potential future links. However, existing reviews lack systematic exploration specifically...

item.title: PlanFlip: Attacking Multi-Agent LLM Systems via Planning-Phase Prompt Injection
item.description: arXiv:2607.16199v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems increasingly rely on a Planner to decompose goals into sub-task sequences that downstream Executor and Critic agents execute and audit. We identify the planning phase as a critical attack surface: a single injection into the...

item.title: Reinforcement Learning-Guided NSGA-II Enhanced with Gray Relational Coefficient for Multi-Objective Optimization: Application to NASDAQ Portfolio Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.16194v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In modern financial markets, decision-makers increasingly rely on quantitative methods to navigate complex trade-offs among multiple, often conflicting objectives. This paper addresses constrained multi-objective optimization (MOO) with an application...

item.title: DocOCR-Eval: A Correction-Based Framework for OCR Tool Selection Without Ground Truth
item.description: arXiv:2607.16203v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document parsing is a foundational step for document understanding tasks such as visual question answering and key information extraction, as it transforms unstructured scanned images into structured representations by extracting textual, visual, and...

item.title: Fully-sensorized smart-eyewear platform for on-device Machine Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.16222v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents ARGO, a smart eyewear platform designed to bridge ergonomic comfort, high computational throughput, and energy efficiency. Unlike cloud-dependent solutions, ARGO leverages the STM32N6 microcontroller and its integrated Neural...

item.title: LLM Unlearning for Cyber Defense: A Survey on Methods, Challenges, and Emerging Threats
item.description: arXiv:2607.16227v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed in security-critical systems across healthcare, finance, education, and decision support, yet their inability to forget creates serious cybersecurity, privacy, and safety risks. Sensitive personal information,...

item.title: Operator-Aware Mixed-Precision Tolerance Calibration for Tensor Kernels
item.description: arXiv:2607.16228v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most tensor-kernel correctness tests go through a fixed-shape all close-style check with hand-picked absolute and relative tolerances. The thresholds are copied across the corpus and rarely revisited. We mine the element-wise error distribution of...

item.title: Multi-level context Modeling for consistent expert selection in Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: arXiv:2607.16427v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) enables efficient scaling of Transformer models by routing tokens to a small subset of experts. However, existing routers typically condition expert selection on shallow or isolated token representations, which often produce...

item.title: RIMS: Preference Optimization via Smoothed Multi-pair Aggregation for Small-Scale LLM Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.16431v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Small-scale language models (SLMs) are attractive for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in resource-constrained settings, but their limited capacity makes them highly sensitive to noisy or spurious retrieved evidence. Existing preference-based...

item.title: Committed Before Reasoning: Behavioral Reproduction and Preliminary Activation-Level Evidence of Answer Pre-Commitment in an Open-Weight LLM
item.description: arXiv:2607.16451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chat models sometimes commit to an answer and then produce reasoning that justifies it rather than deriving it -- even when the answer contradicts a task premise. We study a minimal probe: "I want to wash my car. The car wash is 100 meters away....

item.title: Encoding EEG Signals to Examine Human-Like Next-Word Prediction Behaviour in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.16549v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models (LMs) are trained to excel at predicting the next word in the sequence given prior context, and humans also share this predictability in reading comprehension. Neuroscience research reveals that next-word predictability influences...

item.title: NOWJ@COLIEE 2026: Adaptive Pipelines for Legal Retrieval and Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2607.16603v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents the methodologies and results of the NOWJ team's participation across all five tasks of the COLIEE 2026 competition. For Task 1 (Legal Case Retrieval), we propose a four-stage pipeline comprising candidate filtering, dense...

item.title: [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Grabette: an open system to record robot-manipulation data
Datachannel Unavailable
item.description: Jul 20, 19:13 UTC Resolved - This incident is now resolved. Jul 20, 19:07 UTC Monitoring - We have identified an issue with Realtime datachannel creation failure and have applied a fix. We are currently monitoring the results.

item.title: FakeGit Campaign Uses 7,600 GitHub Repositories to Spread SmartLoader Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered nearly 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories, out of which more than 800 pose as artificial intelligence (AI) skills or Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to deliver a malware family known as SmartLoader as part of an ongoing campaign codenamed FakeGit....

item.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the country’s leading AI companies. David...

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
item.description: A malware operator left its delivery server wide open, and Rapid7 pulled down the whole toolkit: 1,048 files spanning lure templates, filename-spoofing tests, execution experiments, droppers, builder notes, and two campaign chains. One was already live against Windows users in Mexico, delivering an...

item.title: Disruption with GPT 5.3 Codex
item.description: Jul 20, 17:21 UTC Update - The upstream provider continues to return intermittent errors for GPT 5.3 Codex requests. Some users may experience failed or interrupted responses when using this model. Traffic continues to be automatically rerouted to reduce impact, and we are actively working with the...

item.title: Elevated errors for Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 20, 16:16 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 20, 16:07 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 20, 15:09 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 20, 14:41 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Introducing Cosmos 3 Edge
Kimi K3: The open-weights escalation
item.description: The global implications on the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Neo Emerges From Stealth With $100M to Control and Secure Enterprise AI Software
item.description: Neo raised money across seed and Series A funding rounds from Andreessen Horowitz, Bessemer Venture Partners, and others. The post Neo Emerges From Stealth With $100M to Control and Secure Enterprise AI Software appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 20, 13:20 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But...

item.title: Elevated error rates for Opus 4.5
item.description: Jul 20, 10:05 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jul 20, 07:57 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jul 20, 07:03 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
item.description: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also develop their own...

item.title: Incident with GitHub Actions
item.description: Jul 20, 03:03 UTC Update - API Requests is operating normally. Jul 20, 02:43 UTC Update - Issues, API Requests, and Pages have recovered. We are continuing to work on restoring GitHub Actions jobs using self-hosted or larger-hosted runners. Jul 20, 02:23 UTC Update - API Requests is experiencing...

item.title: Elevated errors for GitHub-dependent ChatGPT and Codex workflows
item.description: Status: InvestigatingUsers may experience failures or delays in ChatGPT and Codex workflows that depend on GitHub. This mostly should impact Codex web and code review for PRs on Github. This is related to an active GitHub API partial outage and degraded GitHub Actions....

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 20, 01:46 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 20, 01:45 UTC Update - Git LFS API success rates have returned to normal. We will...

item.title: Elevated errors affecting ChatGPT
item.description: Status: MonitoringService has been restored and we are continuing to monitor.Affected components Voice mode (Operational) Conversations (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) ChatGPT Work (Operational)

item.title: Workers and Pages builds stuck intializing.
item.description: Jul 18, 21:00 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare is aware of an issue initializing Workers & Pages Builds from 22:05 - 22:45 UTC. The problem has now been resolved.
